task.title: Prevent inline compaction of plain block bodies after opening braces
task.summary: The formatter still pulls the first expression in a normal block body onto the same line as the opening brace in some cases.
task.problem: After the 0001-0004 fixes, one clear content regression remains in fixtures/dotfiles/scrubs/lib.nu where a normal if-branch body becomes `if (...) {$env | get $key`. This is not a closure case and not a record literal case; it is a plain block-body compaction problem.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Normal block bodies after `if`, `else if`, `else`, `while`, `for`, `match`, `def`, `export def`, `try`, and `do` do not pull their first body expression onto the opening-brace line unless that style is explicitly chosen and tested.
The exact failing shape from fixtures/dotfiles/scrubs/lib.nu is covered by a regression test.
Legitimate block-opener joins such as `if (...) {` still work.
